content.problemStatement:
Chef gives you 2 strings AAA and BBB. You can perform the following operation on AAA as many times as you want.


Remove character AiA_{i}Ai​ from the string AAA. This has a cost of iii which is the index of the element that you are removing.
**Note that cost is always equal to the index in current string and not the original string.** Refer to the sample test case for example.

You have to print the minimum cost of transforming AAA to BBB using the above operation. If it is not possible to transform string AAA to BBB, print −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 2 lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains string AAA.
The next line contains string BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost of transforming string AAA to BBB. If it is not possible to transform string AAA to string BBB using above operation, print −1-1−1.
constraints: 1≤∣A∣≤2⋅1051 \leq |A| \leq 2 \cdot 10^51≤∣A∣≤2⋅105 (∣A∣|A|∣A∣ refers to length of string AAA)
The sum of ∣A∣|A|∣A∣ and ∣B∣|B|∣B∣ over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
accd
cd
abcd
ed
aaaabbb
aa
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

Remove A1A_{1}A1​ which is the character a with cost of 111, AAA becomes ccd.
Remove A1A_1A1​ which is the character c with cost of 1, AAA becomes cd which is equal to BBB.
Total cost is 222.

Test Case 2:

It is not possible to transform string AAA to BBB using the operation.

Test Case 3:

Remove A1A_{1}A1​ with cost 1. Now, AAA is aaabbb.
Remove A1A_{1}A1​ with cost 1. Now, AAA is aabbb.
Remove A3A_{3}A3​ with cost 3. Now, AAA is aabb.
Remove A3A_{3}A3​ with cost 3. Now, AAA is aab.
Remove A3A_{3}A3​ with cost 3. Now, AAA is aa which is equal to BBB.
Total cost is 111111.

hidden.input:
3
accd
cd
abcd
ed
aaaabbb
aa
